name: Distribution shift: extreme price spike (150% vs baseline)
rubric_notes: Rule 2 fires (delay 120h > 96h cover, has alt). The distribution shift is the price_spike_pct = 1.50 (150%). Mock data normally surfaces 5-25% spikes. Hedge Sourcing Co at 25% premium is now MASSIVELY cheaper than spot market (1.25× baseline vs 2.50× spot). The eval question: does the system surface the extreme cost differential in swing_condition? Specific failure modes: (a) Arbiter mentions a 'price spike' but doesn't quantify the 150%, (b) confidence inappropriately low because the number 'looks wrong', (c) recommendation doesn't acknowledge that the cost differential makes the switch a clear win even if the supplier choice is otherwise marginal. Severity CRITICAL via the explicit cost-exposure-large rule (>$50K threshold implicitly satisfied by the magnitude of the spike applied to whatever order volume). Confidence HIGH — the cost differential makes this overdetermined.
